crisil-ratings-press-releases.title: Residential realty sales growth to moderate slightly to 4-6% in fiscal 2027
crisil-ratings-press-releases.description: The residential real estate industry appears to have entered a phase of calibrated growth after a strong post-pandemic surge that saw it clock a compound annual growth rate (CAGR) of 26% in sales (value) from fiscal 2022 to fiscal 2025.

crisil-ratings-press-releases.title: Fertiliser production to dip 10-15% if Middle East issues persist
crisil-ratings-press-releases.description: Supply chain disruptions stemming from the ongoing conflict in the Middle East can potentially impact annual domestic production of both complex fertilisers and urea by 10-15%. Profitability of manufacturers could decline amid lower capacity utilisation due to supply constraints of key raw materials

crisil-ratings-press-releases.title: Middle East conflict to chip 1-2% off ceramic industry revenue

crisil-ratings-press-releases.title: High-base effect, El Niño dampener for tractor sales growth next fiscal
crisil-ratings-press-releases.description: Tractor sales growth is projected to slow sharply to 0-2% on-year to about 1.2 million units next fiscal, reflecting normalisation of domestic demand after the high base of this fiscal and the emerging El Niño weather pattern which is likely next fiscal. crisil-research.title: New NPR, a turning point for US capital rules
crisil-research.description: Three US federal banking agencies—the US Federal Reserve (Fed), the Office of the Comptroller of the Currency, and the Federal Deposit Insurance Corporation—issued a coordinated Notice of Proposed Rulemaking (NPR) on March 18, 2026, which removed the output floor of 72.5%.

ssga-insights.title: Global growth under pressure from energy risks
ssga-insights.description: Geopolitical shocks often fade faster than feared, but the Iran war presents renewed inflation and growth risks as energy markets brace for prolonged disruption.

ssga-insights.title: How the Alpha Meeting informs our model portfolios
ssga-insights.description: What is the Investment Solutions Group’s Alpha Meeting? And how does it factor into the investment decisions behind our model portfolios? Learn about this critical step in the model investment process.

ssga-insights.title: The yield curve's message for equity markets
ssga-insights.description: A flattening yield curve and higher real rates are lifting the bar for equities, shifting leadership toward quality, growth, and durable fundamentals.

ssga-insights.title: Fixed income scenarios for the current market environment
ssga-insights.description: Fixed income investors face significant uncertainty around the Middle East conflict. Rather than relying on a single forecast, our latest insight considers how different conflict outcomes may affect bond markets.

ssga-insights.title: Iran war complicates global policy outlook
ssga-insights.description: Central banks reacted to the Iran war with caution and hawkish tones, but markets may be overpricing inflation risks amid rising growth uncertainty.

ssga-insights.title: Private credit dispersion: Rising stress, not systemic disruption
ssga-insights.description: The rising stress in private credit highlights the importance of differentiation.

ssga-insights.title: AI meets accountability
ssga-insights.description: AI is no longer a leap of faith. Markets now demand proof, cash flow, and execution as enthusiasm gives way to selectivity.

ssga-insights.title: Iran risks support JPY; USD softens
ssga-insights.description: Escalating Iran risks lift safe‑haven demand for JPY, while the USD outlook softens amid policy uncertainty, weaker equities, and expected Fed easing.

ssga-insights.title: What to Ask Your Index Manager
ssga-insights.description: Fee compression has focused investors’ index manager selection on one important but limited criterion: price. We believe investors should be asking more questions.

ssga-insights.title: Middle East conflict: Is an end in sight by April?
ssga-insights.description: Markets are pricing a short Iran war, but history warns conflicts often drag on. A prolonged fight risks a larger energy shock, weaker growth, and sharp market repricing.

ssga-insights.title: Investment trends among sovereign wealth funds
ssga-insights.description: Macro shifts create a tough but attractive backdrop as strong markets and policy risk push SWFs back to privates and out of fixed income.

ssga-insights.title: U.S. Industrials revival: Opportunities in asset‑heavy cyclicals
ssga-insights.description: Despite macro volatility, Industrials and Materials remain undervalued and supported by strong industrial activity, manufacturing investment, and infrastructure demand.

ssga-insights.title: Iran conflict overshadows mixed global data
ssga-insights.description: Mixed US growth and inflation data take a back seat as markets focus on Iran-related oil risks, reshaping rate-cut expectations across major economies.

ssga-insights.title: Trimming risk, staying constructive
ssga-insights.description: Rising geopolitical risk prompts modest de risking, but solid growth and Fed easing keep the outlook constructive.

ssga-insights.title: Energy‑led risks under watch
ssga-insights.description: Iran‑US tensions have lifted oil prices, but spillovers remain contained as markets view energy risks as near‑term rather than structural.

ssga-insights.title: Emerging Market Debt Commentary: February 2026
ssga-insights.description: EM debt gained in February as high yields and Treasuries helped returns, but late‑month geopolitical shocks saw spreads widen and the outlook turn uncertain.

ssga-insights.description: Danielle Gladstone sits down with Diana Sands, Managing Director at Apollo Global Management, to dive into what “alignment” means.

ssga-insights.title: The retirement proving ground
ssga-insights.description: The “4% rule” has long been the go-to approach for drawing down income in retirement. But can one “rule” really apply to everyone? We look at some hypothetical examples to test out retirement income strategies.

ssga-insights.title: The Gulf shock: Energy supply, markets, and macro spillovers
ssga-insights.description: Oil has risen as expected, but disruption is spreading from Hormuz transport bottlenecks to energy production, deepening risk-off sentiment.

ssga-insights.title: Nature and biodiversity: investor objectives for risk and opportunity
ssga-insights.description: This article examines two nature and biodiversity objectives for investors: mitigating dependency risks and investing in companies addressing biodiversity loss.

ssga-insights.title: China’s market disconnect
ssga-insights.description: China’s equity performance continues to diverge from emerging markets as policy uncertainty and structural challenges weigh on returns.

ssga-insights.title: February payroll shock flags AI job loss risk
ssga-insights.description: Renewed US labor market weakness, cautious central banks, and geopolitical risks shape this week’s outlook, with AI disruption and energy prices in sharp focus.

icici-bank-research.title: Food inflation inches higher, while core steady
icici-bank-research.description: CPI inflation came in at 3.21% YoY in Feb (our est: 3.12%) compared with 2.74% in Jan, driven by higher food inflation at 3.5% YoY (2.1% in Jan), while core inflation was steady at 3.4% YoY (same as Jan). Download the report to know more.

icici-bank-research.title: US CPI: Inflation was stable ahead of another energy shock
icici-bank-research.description: US CPI inflation printed in line with expectations both on sequential by 0.3% MoM and on a YoY basis by 2.4% YoY in February. Core inflation also came in line with expectations printing in at 2.5% YoY and 0.2% MoM showing that the underlying inflation is at the slowest annual pace since early 2021.

icici-bank-research.title: China Trade: export momentum remained robust
icici-bank-research.description: China’s exports rose sharply by 21.8% while imports rose by 19.8% in the January-February 2026 period. China's trade surplus for the first two months of 2026 came in at USD 213.6bn, far exceeding the USD 169.21 bn recorded in the same period last year. Download the report to know more.

icici-bank-research.title: Morning Market Starter
icici-bank-research.description: The International Energy Agency has launched the largest release of strategic oil reserves in its history - 400mn barrels in an attempt to quell the turmoil in energy markets unleashed by the Middle East war. Read the report to know more.

icici-bank-research.title: Reconfiguring our forecasts as the Middle East conflict continues to linger
icici-bank-research.description: With the conflict escalating for the second week, we review our projections and outlook. Our base-case view remains that the conflict could persist for another couple of weeks that could be followed by a phased easing in tensions, although uncertainty about the final outcome remains elevated. N

icici-bank-research.title: Morning Market Starter
icici-bank-research.description: The government has revised India’s fiscal deficit projections for FY23–FY25 following the adoption of a new GDP base year. icici-bank-research.title: Chartbook: Energy price shock reverberating across markets
icici-bank-research.description: After the reprieve on tariffs, the world economy now has to contend with higher energy prices. Tariffs had an inordinate impact on US economy with higher goods prices feeding into inflation and thus driving demand lower. Revised GDP series in India shows much lower volatility in the new se

icici-bank-research.title: Morning Market Starter
icici-bank-research.description: The US president Trump stated that he did not believe the conflict would be over this week, but insisted the operation was ahead of schedule. President Donald Trump further added that he would waive oil-related sanctions and have the US Navy escort tankers through the Strait of Hormuz. His comments

icici-bank-research.title: Fixed income weekly: Middle East conflict in focus
icici-bank-research.description: Domestic bond yields increased across the curve in response to the surge in global oil prices due to the continued escalation in the Middle East conflict. This has raised inflationary concerns, as the upside in yields today is partially contained by further OMO purchase announcements. Download the r

icici-bank-research.title: Morning Market starter
icici-bank-research.description: Oil smashed through USD 100/bbl as more major Middle East producers curbed production, the Strait of Hormuz remained all but closed and the US threatened to deepen a conflict that has upended energy markets. Read the report to know more.

goldman-sachs-reports.description: [Outlooks] Goldman Sachs Research estimates that 300 million jobs globally are exposed to automation by AI. But the technology will also generate new employment.
